page: Amazon Web Services m7g.4xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores based on the ARMv8 arm64 architecture, specifically utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V1 model running at 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and a 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations position the m7g.4xlarge primarily in the average performance tier for general-purpose workloads. Its single-core and multi-core CPU capabilities, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, fall within the middle 50% of tested systems. Notably, the instance demonstrates top-tier memory performance, with a PassMark memory score in the top 10% and a strong large-block memory write bandwidth of 99.53 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25%. Conversely, cryptographic performance is a relative bottleneck, with the PassMark CPU encryption test registering in the bottom 25%. LLM inference speeds are average for small models but drop to the bottom 25% for prompt processing on medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides a resource-dense profile suitable for general-purpose applications without the premium associated with x86 hardware. Given its balanced CPU-to-memory ratio, high memory write bandwidth, and average database operations performance, this instance is well-suited for application servers, static web serving, software compilation, and redis-based caching layers. It is less optimal for heavy cryptographic tasks or medium-to-large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7g.4xlarge is an ARM-based general-purpose server powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz, 64.0 GB of memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps, managed via the Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows average CPU performance across single-core and multi-core tests, but highlights top-tier memory performance, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10% and strong large-block memory write speeds. However, cryptographic operations represent a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%. This instance is suited for balanced workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and caching databases, offering a resource-dense, cost-efficient alternative to traditional x86 hardware.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.4xlarge is an ARM-based general-purpose server featuring 16 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V1 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory performance for balanced enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.4xlarge is a general-purpose ARM server with 16 dedicated cores, 64 GB RAM, and high-tier memory write bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose server offering dedicated physical cores and top-tier memory performance for balanced enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 cores at 2.6 GHz
Equipped with 64 GB of system memory with top-tier write bandwidth
Delivers 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for web serving, software compilation, and caching databases
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage
